title: New race: podman rm -t 0 --all --force: container does not exist in database
body:
This is starting to hit us badly in CI. Super-trivial to reproduce:
```console
$ while :;do bin/podman run --rm -d --name foo quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 top; bin/podman kill foo; bin/podman rm -t 0 --all --force || break;done
...
4642ba927711e9f396ee77eff476ec735e1459298193991acc36e58897d2b9b9
foo
b2f95effbf7ba61d91c415d9d3ff9796fba6ea8e30779113167f01a3590ffb28
foo
Error: container b2f95effbf7ba61d91c415d9d3ff9796fba6ea8e30779113167f01a3590ffb28 does not exist in database: no such container
```
For reasons not clear to me, it seems to take 10-15 iterations to fail as rootless, but 1-2 root.

CI failures happen unpredictably, in no specific test, so they're very hard to find and classify. 

### [sys] 177 podman kill - print IDs or raw input
* fedora-35 : sys podman fedora-35 root host
  * PR #12725
    * [12-31 10:12](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5935143033503744/html/sys-podman-fedora-35-root-host.log.html#t--00177)

Another one just now in an in-flight PR: [fedora 35 root host](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4508811338711040/html/sys-podman-fedora-35-root-host.log.html)

comments.body: I do not understand why we run `podman run --rm ...` followed by `podman rm ...`. Now two processes will try to remove the container (the cleanup process spawned when conmon exits and podman rm). If the cleanup process is faster, `podman rm` will fail because it cannot find the container anymore.
Although this is `podman rm --all`, so I think we should ignore such errors.

>I do not understand why we run podman run --rm ... followed by podman rm ...

Test `teardown()` runs `rm -t 0 --all --force` as a standard way to clean up and restore to known state.
This does strike me as a bug in `podman rm --all`, yeah - classic TOCTOU between database lookup of containers and trying to remove them.
We are attempting to catch this error everywhere I looked yesterday.  So this must be burried somewhere deep.

The error is here I believe https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/infra/abi/containers.go#L333-L346

Can you change your script to add --ignore, should fix the problem.
If the error comes from getContainersByContext then ignoring the error is useless since it will only try to remove the containers that were given by name not **all**
